evidence.fact: When a patient's urgency level changes, the queue automatically re-sorts to maintain correct ordering.
evidence.user_story_span: the system to order the queue

evidence.claim: Queue items have an urgency level.
evidence.fact: The system can retrieve the patient with the highest urgency and earliest arrival from the queue.
evidence.user_story_span: items, each with an urgency level
evidence.fact_span: the patient with the highest urgency

evidence.fact: The waiting list is organized using descending severity and ascending check-in timestamp.
evidence.user_story_span: items, each with an urgency level and a timestamp

evidence.claim: When the interaction occurs, the system orders the queue first by urgency (descending), then by time (ascending).
evidence.fact: The waiting list is organized using descending severity and ascending check-in timestamp.
evidence.user_story_span: order the queue by urgency then time
evidence.fact_span: organized using descending severity and ascending check-in timestamp
